1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I serve to build up the faith of God's chosen people and to help them know the truth that leads to a godly life.
This faith and truth give us the hope of eternal life. God, who never lies, promised this life before time began.
And at the right time God made his message known through the preaching that God our Saviour trusted to me.
To Titus, my true son in the faith we share: May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Saviour give you grace and peace.
I left you in Crete so that you could put right the things that still needed work, and choose leaders for the church in every town, as I told you.
A church leader must have nothing against him. He must be faithful to his wife, and his children must be believers who are not wild or disobedient.
Since a leader is trusted with God's work, he must have nothing against him. He must not be proud, quick-tempered, a heavy drinker, violent, or greedy for money.
Instead, he must welcome strangers, love what is good, and be wise, fair, holy, and self-controlled.
He must hold firmly to the true message he was taught. Then he can encourage others with sound teaching and show those who argue against it where they are wrong.
For there are many people who will not obey. They talk nonsense and lead others away from the truth, especially those who say everyone must follow the Jewish law.
These people must be stopped. They are upsetting whole families by teaching wrong things, and they do it for dishonest money.
Even one of their own prophets said: The people of Crete are always liars, cruel animals, and lazy people who only want to eat.
This is true. So correct them strongly, so that their faith will be sound.
Tell them to stop paying attention to Jewish myths and to the rules of people who turn away from the truth.
To those who are pure, everything is pure. But to those who are sinful and do not believe, nothing is pure; even their minds and their hearts are unclean.
They say they know God, but their actions say no. They are disgusting and disobedient, and they are no good for doing anything right.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: But you, Titus, must teach what agrees with sound teaching.
Teach the older men to be serious, worthy of respect, and wise, and to be strong in faith, in love, and in patience.
In the same way, teach the older women to live in a holy way. They must not tell lies about others or drink too much wine. Instead, they must teach what is good.
Then they can train the younger women to love their husbands and their children,
to be wise and pure, to take care of their homes, to be kind, and to obey their husbands. Then no one can say evil things about the word of God.
In the same way, encourage the younger men to be wise and self-controlled.
In everything, you yourself must be an example of doing good. When you teach, be honest and serious.
Say what is true, so that no one can prove it wrong. Then anyone who is against us will be ashamed, because they have nothing bad to say about us.
Teach servants to obey their masters and to please them in everything. They must not talk back
or steal from them. Instead, they must show that they can be fully trusted. In this way they will make the teaching about God our Saviour beautiful in every way.
For the grace of God has come to save all people.
That grace teaches us to say no to sinful living and to the wrong desires of this world, and to live wisely, doing what is right and pleasing to God,
while we wait for the blessed hope: the glorious coming of our great God and Saviour, Jesus Christ.
Jesus gave himself for us, to set us free from all sin and to make us his own special people, eager to do good.
Teach these things. Encourage the people and correct them with full authority. Do not let anyone look down on you.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Remind the people to obey the rulers and authorities, to listen to them, and to be ready to do good.
Tell them not to say evil things about anyone, and not to argue or fight. Instead, they must be gentle and kind to everyone.

At one time we too were foolish and disobedient. We were fooled, and we were slaves to all kinds of wrong desires and pleasures. We were full of hate and jealousy. People hated us, and we hated each other.
But then the kindness and love of God our Saviour appeared.
God saved us — not because of the good things we had done, but because of his mercy. He saved us by washing us clean and giving us new life through the Holy Spirit.
God poured out the Holy Spirit on us freely through Jesus Christ our Saviour.
By his grace God made us right with himself, so that we could receive what he promised: the hope of eternal life.
This is a true saying. I want you to keep teaching these things, so that those who trust in God will be careful to do good. These things are good and helpful for everyone.
But stay away from foolish arguments, from family-tree lists, and from fights and quarrels about the law. They are useless and a waste of time.
If someone causes division, warn him once, and then a second time. After that, have nothing to do with him.
You know that such a person has turned away from the truth and is sinning. He has judged himself.
As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, because I have decided to spend the winter there.
Do everything you can to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way, so that they have everything they need.
Our people must learn to spend their time doing good and meeting the real needs of others, so that their lives are not wasted.
Everyone here with me sends you greetings. Greet our friends in the faith. May grace be with you all. Amen.
